nodes.text: Hey, sorry — I know, I know. I was gonna clean up first. Hold on.

responses.text: It's fine. What can I help you with?

nodes.text: Appreciate it. Tommy Briggs. Monthly pickup — lisinopril. I meant to swing home first but traffic on the bypass was just brutal tonight. Figured I'd just stop.

responses.text: The bypass is always bad around five. Let me look you up.
No problem. Can I see your ID?

nodes.text: You could say that. Three-car job, one of them had a cracked block nobody mentioned. Four hours of overtime right there. I hold my hands up — sorry, I swear I'm not usually like this.

responses.text: No need to apologize. Who are you picking up for?
Sounds like you earned a drink. Can I get your name?

nodes.text: Yeah, I should've taken Cedar Street but I always forget. Tommy Briggs. B-R-I-G-G-S. Dr. Webb's my guy. You need the ID?
Just me. Lisinopril, ten milligrams. Dr. Webb put me on it about eighteen months ago. Blood pressure. I keep telling myself I'll cut back on the red meat but here we are.

responses.text: One step at a time. I'll pull up your file.

nodes.text: Tommy Briggs. And yeah, honestly, I'm grabbing one of those energy drinks before I head out. Don't judge me.

responses.text: After a day like that? Go ahead. ID first though.

nodes.text: Yeah, yeah — here. Sorry about the fingerprints on it, I tried to wipe it on my shirt but that didn't help much.
